summary: ftsRowID swapped from 31-multiplier polynomial hash to fnv64a (stdlib hash/fnv) with 0x00 delimiter between scope and id.
reason: Greptile P2: Bento IDs are short and numerically similar ("1464", "1465", "70064351"). The polynomial hash has weak avalanche on these inputs; a collision on the truncated 63-bit rowid would cause DELETE WHERE rowid=? to silently nuke the wrong FTS row, leaving stale/missing search results. fnv64a is order-sensitive on a flat byte stream and has much stronger distribution; the delimiter prevents scope/id boundary aliasing.
